rules_added: step_by_step must provide exactly one consolidated procedure
step_by_step must not mix distinct documented procedures
step_by_step must continue until the actual user goal is reached
step_by_step answer must stay a short lead-in and must not repeat numbered or listed steps
step_by_step procedural content belongs in steps

intermediate_v2.interpretation: Initial v2 hardening fixed one old duplication case but still allowed answer-plus-steps double emission, which created two new duplication regressions.

improved_cases.rationale: The old answer repeated the same 3-step procedure twice. The v3 answer emits one clean procedure only once.

side_effects_outside_step_by_step.summary: No clarification, faq_direct_answer, troubleshooting or uncertainty_escalation case flipped decision in the v3 rerun.

answer_quality_vs_judge_reaction.primary_interpretation: The measurable gain is concentrated in one former step_by_step duplication reject and comes from changed answer text, not from judge drift on the same answer.
supporting_points: The rerun reused the exact same 32-job selection and the same saved user simulations from v1.
The only decision flip from baseline to v3 is eval::step_by_step::0002, where the visible answer changed from duplicated procedure to a single clean procedure.

conclusion.why_not_done: The concentrated duplication and mixed-procedure failure modes are meaningfully reduced, but hard completeness cases still fail and remain the highest-risk class.
conclusion.next_step: Target the remaining completeness blocker directly: for step_by_step, prefer refusing to imply a finished procedure when the excerpt stops before the actual goal state, and explicitly force the last goal-achieving step or a narrower answer scope.
